rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By John And Jill Kinney To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 4, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANTS JOHN KINNEY AND JILL KINNEY'S Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: CENTRAL GARDENS, INC. VS. ROBIN DEWAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Prohibitory Injunction To Maintain The Status Quo
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 4, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CENTRAL GARDENS, INC.'S Motion For A Prohibitory Injunction To Maintain The Status Quo. Granted in part. Pending further order of the court and entry of a final judgment, Ms. Dewar and Mr. O'Leary are restrained from encumbering or selling all or any portion of any of the real properties they currently own other than for the purposes of payment of attorney's fees for this litigation and to improve, maintain or repair the condition of any such properties. Balance of equities favors granting such an injunction, especially since Ms. Dewar appears to admit that she engaged in conduct for which she is liable to the plaintiff, the trial date was recently continued to accommodate defendants, and the only harm identified by defendants has been addressed by the terms of the injunction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC, IN THE PUBLIC VS. HARBOR FREIGHT TOOLS USA, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Initial Responses To Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday May 4, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC.'S, Motion To Compel Initial Responses To Plaintiff Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARK HOOSHMAND VS. KATHARINA WOHL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Wohls Attendance At Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 4, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF MARK HOOSHMAND'S Motion To Compel Defendant Wohls Attendance At Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel. Denied without prejudice to be refiled, if necessary, after the Court rules on plaintiff's demurrer set for hearing on September 22, 2015. The Court's decision on the demurrer has the potential to be dispositive of the entire lawsuit. The Court has authority to order discovery while pleadings are under attack. However, the moving party has not made a sufficient showing of necessity for a deposition to proceed at this time. The debt which is the subject of the action may have been extinguished by the defendant's Chapter 7 bankruptcy and there has been no order of the Bankruptcy Court providing the plaintiff with a Relief from Stay so as to allow the action to have been initially filed or to proceed. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
